name: Sarah Johnson
summary: 27-year-old female presenting with chronic fatigue and pallor, diagnosed with Iron Deficiency Anemia after laboratory confirmation. Initiated on oral iron supplementation with symptomatic improvement.

conditions.name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
conditions.notes: Presumed etiology: heavy menstrual bleeding. Oral iron supplementation initiated.

medications.name: Ferrous Sulfate
medications.notes: To be taken with Vitamin C for better absorption. Advised on potential GI side effects.

visits.chief_complaint: Persistent fatigue and weakness for several months
visits.narrative:
Patient presents with a 6-month history of worsening fatigue, generalized weakness, and occasional dizziness. Reports feeling constantly tired, impacting daily activities and work performance. Denies fever, weight loss, or changes in bowel habits. Reports heavy menstrual periods which may contribute to blood loss.

Physical exam reveals conjunctival pallor and pale nail beds. Heart rate 88 bpm, regular. Lungs clear to auscultation. No lymphadenopathy. Ordered CBC, serum iron panel (iron, ferritin, TIBC). Started empirical Ferrous Sulfate 325mg daily, pending lab results. Advised to increase dietary iron intake.

Patient returns for review of labs. CBC shows microcytic hypochromic anemia (Hgb 9.5 g/dL, MCV 72 fL). Iron panel reveals significantly low ferritin (8 ng/mL) and low serum iron (25 mcg/dL) with elevated TIBC (450 mcg/dL), confirming iron deficiency anemia.

Diagnosis of Iron Deficiency Anemia confirmed. Discussed probable etiology related to heavy menstrual bleeding. Reinforced importance of consistent Ferrous Sulfate use. Advised to take iron with Vitamin C and away from dairy/calcium. Educated on symptoms of improvement and potential side effects. Will recheck labs in 8-10 weeks.

Patient returns for a routine check-up, approximately 12 weeks after starting iron supplementation. Reports significant improvement in fatigue and energy levels. No longer experiencing dizziness. Menstrual bleeding still heavy but has not worsened.

Physical exam shows improved conjunctival color. Repeat labs demonstrate good response to therapy: Hemoglobin increased to 12.8 g/dL, Ferritin to 45 ng/mL. Iron stores are replenishing, and anemia is resolving. Advised to continue Ferrous Sulfate for another 3-4 months to fully replete iron stores and then consider reducing frequency or discontinuing based on future lab results. Referred to gynecology for evaluation of heavy menstrual bleeding.

referrals.reason: Gynecology consultation for evaluation of heavy menstrual bleeding contributing to iron deficiency anemia.
